display.purpose: Hero names and major headings only. Never used in body copy.

headline.purpose: Section titles (H2). Unified scale across all pages.

body.purpose: Running text, descriptions, project summaries. Always in Warm Silver / Soft Graphite, never White Stone.

label.purpose: Eyebrow text, button labels, nav items, form labels. Always uppercase with wide tracking.

shadows.name: card-base-rest
shadows.purpose: Default card shadow. Subtle, nearly invisible. Light mode: 0.04 opacity.

shadows.name: card-base-hover
shadows.purpose: Card hover shadow. Lifts the card 6px and deepens shadow. Light mode: 0.10 opacity.

shadows.name: card-minimal
shadows.purpose: Light ambient shadow for minimal containers (control cluster, card-minimal). Light mode: 0.04.

shadows.name: section-shell
shadows.purpose: Broad, soft shadow for large section wrappers. Light mode: 0.04.

shadows.name: mobile-drawer
shadows.purpose: Deep focused shadow for the mobile navigation overlay. Highest shadow value.

shadows.name: project-card-hover
shadows.purpose: Hover shadow for elevated project cards. More pronounced than card-base.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions (hover, focus).

motion.name: ease-in-out
motion.purpose: Easing for animated elements like the mobile drawer and carousel track.

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Hover transitions, focus-ring changes, chip interactions.

motion.name: duration-medium
motion.purpose: Theme toggle, border color transitions, card transformations.

motion.name: fade-in-up
motion.purpose: Standard entrance animation: element fades in while moving up 16px.

breakpoints.name: md
breakpoints.purpose: Tablet. Desktop nav visible, grid switches to 2-column.

breakpoints.name: 2xl
breakpoints.purpose: Ultra-wide. Content remains centered at max-width.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Solid amber CTA button. Used for primary actions: view projects, contact, submit. High contrast amber fill on near-black background.

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary outline button with transparent fill and subtle border. For secondary CTAs on the homepage and section transitions.

components.name: Ghost Link Button
components.description: Minimal text-only button with amber accent. Used for download CV and other tertiary actions. No border or background — relies entirely on typography and color.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-ghost">DESCARGAR CV<svg class="ds-icon-right" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" width="16" height="16"><path d="M7 17L17 7M7 7h10v10"/></svg></button>

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Text input with elevated dark background and amber focus ring. Used in the contact form for name, email, and subject fields. Label sits above the field.

components.name: Textarea
components.description: Multi-line textarea matching input styling. Used for the message field in the contact form. Vertical resize only, same focus treatment as inputs.

components.name: Navigation Link (Desktop)
components.description: Desktop navigation link with animated underline on hover. Uppercase, tight tracking, warm silver default shifting to amber on interaction.
components.html: PROYECTOSSOBRE MÍCONTACTO

components.name: Chip / Tag (Outline)
components.description: Small pill-shaped tag for technology labels. Transparent background with border. Used in project cards to display tech stack tags.

components.name: Project Card
components.description: Elevated project card with amber-tinted hover border and lift effect. Used in the project directory and homepage featured section. 32px padding with header, body, and footer zones.
components.html:

Real-time collaborative logging platform for dev teams.

components.css: .ds-card-project{display:flex;flex-direction:column;height:100%;border:1px solid #27272a;background:#14141a;padding:2rem;transition:transform .3s,box-shadow .3s,border-color .3s}.ds-card-project:hover{transform:translateY(-6px);border-color:color-mix(in srgb,#f59e0b 50%,transparent);box-shadow:0 18px 40px rgba(2,6,23,0.18)}.ds-card-header{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:flex-start;gap:1rem}.ds-card-year{font-family:'JetBrains Mono',monospace;font-size:0.625rem;color:#9ca3af;white-space:nowrap}.ds-card-title{font-family:'Instrument Serif',Georgia,serif;font-size:1.875rem;font-weight:700;line-height:1.1;letter-spacing:-0.02em;color:#fafafa;margin:2rem 0 0.75rem}.ds-card-desc{font-family:'Work Sans',system-ui,sans-serif;font-size:1rem;line-height:1.7;color:#a1a1aa;max-width:60ch}.ds-card-footer{margin-top:2rem;border-top:1px solid color-mix(in srgb,#27272a 10%,transparent);padding-top:1.5rem}.ds-card-link{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:0.5rem;font-family:'Work Sans',system-ui,sans-serif;font-size:0.75rem;font-weight:700;letter-spacing:0.1em;text-transform:uppercase;color:#f59e0b;text-decoration:none;transition:color .15s}.ds-card-link:hover{color:#d97706}.ds-card-link svg{transition:transform .15s}.ds-card-link:hover svg{transform:translateX(2px)}

components.name: Carousel Control
components.description: Circular control button for the project image carousel. Blends amber on hover. Chevron icon inlined. Used in case study pages.
components.html: 1 / 3

components.name: Language Switcher
components.description: Pill-shaped bilingual toggle for ES/EN. Active state uses elevated background with subtle shadow. Inactive state is transparent with semi-transparent text.

narrative.overview:
This portfolio is a room with controlled lighting, noble materials, and precise blueprints. Every surface is deliberate — nothing is here because a template put it here. The studio is dark by default: deep near-black walls that push content forward, with amber as the only source of warmth, like monitor light reflecting off a desk at dusk. The designer works in this room, and the room itself proves the work.

The system practices what it preaches. A Full Stack Developer portfolio cannot look like it was generated — the portfolio IS the proof of craft. Every component, transition, and layout choice must feel intentional, not inherited. Dark-first is not a fashion statement here; it is the atmosphere of the room. Light mode is given equal care, with warm parchment tones and a burnished amber that preserves the same hierarchy and personality in daylight.
keyCharacteristics: Dark-first, dual-mode. Near-black void background is the default. Light mode switches to warm parchment with its own amber accent, not inverted colors.
Single accent restraint. Amber (OKLCH ~0.70 0.158 74) is the only chromatic color. It occupies ≤10% of any given screen. Its rarity is the point.
Responsive elevation. Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows appear only on hover or interaction — quiet in repose, responsive to touch.
Editorial typography contrast. Serif display (Instrument Serif) for hierarchy and personality; sans body (Work Sans) for readability. The pairing is the primary aesthetic move.
Micro-interactions with purpose. Motion communicates state change and hierarchy. It never decorates.

rules.name: The Monitor Glow Rule
rules.body: Amber occupies ≤10% of any given screen. It is the flash of a monitor in a dark room, not a floodlight. The accent is always the smallest area, always the highest contrast.

rules.name: The Two-Amber Rule
rules.body: Dark mode amber and light mode amber are NOT the same color. Dark mode wants a bright, warm amber (#f59e0b) against near-black. Light mode wants a deeper, more saturated amber (#b45309) against warm parchment. If you swap them, one mode will look washed out or harsh.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: No surface has a shadow at rest unless it is structurally necessary (section-shell, mobile drawer). Hover shadows must feel like a physical response, not a design tick.

rules.name: The Anti-Generic Rule
rules.body: If someone could look at this interface and say 'AI made that' without doubt, it has failed. No purple/blue gradients, no glassmorphism, no template cards, no numbered section markers as scaffolding.

dos: Do use Monitor Amber as the single accent across all interactive states — buttons, links, focus rings, active indicators. If a second color appears, it must be an error state (red) or a neutral.
Do keep body text in Warm Silver / Soft Graphite, never White Stone / Iron Ink. Reserve maximum contrast for headings.
Do animate with purpose: state transitions (150ms fast), entrance reveals (500ms fade-in-up with stagger), theme toggles (250ms). Each animation must have a prefers-reduced-motion: reduce fallback.
Do use the tonal layering system for depth before adding shadows. Surfaces stack: Void → Slate → Shadow Blue. Shadows are the last resort, not the first.
Do keep chips and labels tight — 10px uppercase bold with tight tracking. These are the 'instrument panel' of the interface: small, dense, informative.
Do cap display text at 6rem (~96px) max. Larger than that is shouting, not designing.
Do use text-wrap: balance on all h1–h3 headings for even line lengths, and text-wrap: pretty on body text to reduce orphans.
donts: Don't use purple gradients, neon accents, blue links, or glassmorphism. These are AI-generic developer portfolio tells that the system explicitly rejects.
Don't do scroll-hijacking, excessive animations, or agency-style overdesign. The studio is calm — motion communicates, it doesn't perform.
Don't use numbered section markers (01 / 02 / 03) as default scaffolding above every section. Numbers earn their place only when the content IS a sequence.
Don't use the tiny uppercase tracked eyebrow above every section heading as a default. One deliberate kicker is voice; an eyebrow on every section is AI grammar.
Don't use side-stripe borders (border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent on cards or callouts). Use full borders, background tints, or nothing.
Don't use gradient text. Emphasis is achieved through weight, size, and color, not decorative gradients.
Don't use cards when a simpler layout would do. Cards are the most overused pattern in developer portfolios. Nested cards are always wrong.
Don't swap dark-mode amber (#f59e0b) into light mode or light-mode amber (#b45309) into dark mode. Each mode has its own amber.
Don't forget light mode. It is not dark mode inverted — it has its own amber, its own neutrals, its own shadow opacities.
